NKJV.1: Praise the LORD! For it is good to sing praises to our God; For it is pleasant, and praise is beautiful.
NKJV.2: The LORD builds up Jerusalem;  He gathers together the outcasts of Israel.
NKJV.3: He heals the brokenhearted And binds up their wounds.
NKJV.4: He counts the number of the stars; He calls them all by name.
NKJV.5: Great is our Lord, and mighty in power; His understanding is infinite.
NKJV.6: The LORD lifts up the humble; He casts the wicked down to the ground.
NKJV.7: Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving; Sing praises on the harp to our God,
NKJV.8: Who covers the heavens with clouds, Who prepares rain for the earth, Who makes grass to grow on the mountains.
NKJV.9: He gives to the beast its food, And to the young ravens that cry.
NKJV.10: He does not delight in the strength of the horse; He takes no pleasure in the legs of a man.
NKJV.11: The LORD takes pleasure in those who fear Him, In those who hope in His mercy.
NKJV.12: Praise the LORD, O Jerusalem! Praise your God, O Zion!
NKJV.13: For He has strengthened the bars of your gates; He has blessed your children within you.
NKJV.14: He makes peace in your borders, And fills you with the finest wheat.
NKJV.15: He sends out His command to the earth; His word runs very swiftly.
NKJV.16: He gives snow like wool; He scatters the frost like ashes;
NKJV.17: He casts out His hail like morsels; Who can stand before His cold?
NKJV.18: He sends out His word and melts them; He causes His wind to blow, and the waters flow.
NKJV.19: He declares His word to Jacob,  His statutes and His judgments to Israel.
NKJV.20: He has not dealt thus with any nation; And as for His judgments, they have not known them. Praise the LORD!

KJV.1: Praise<S>1984</S> ye the LORD<S>3050</S>: for it is good<S>2896</S> to sing praises<S>2167</S> unto our God<S>430</S>; for it is pleasant<S>5273</S>; and praise<S>8416</S> is comely<S>5000</S>.
KJV.2: The LORD<S>3068</S> doth build up<S>1129</S> Jerusalem<S>3389</S>: he gathereth together<S>3664</S> the outcasts<S>1760</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>.
KJV.3: He healeth<S>7495</S> the broken<S>7665</S> in heart<S>3820</S>, and bindeth up<S>2280</S> their wounds<S>6094</S>. <sup>wounds: Heb. griefs</sup>
KJV.4: He telleth<S>4487</S> the number<S>4557</S> of the stars<S>3556</S>; he calleth<S>7121</S> them all by their names<S>8034</S>.
KJV.5: Great<S>1419</S> is our Lord<S>113</S>, and of great<S>7227</S> power<S>3581</S>: his understanding<S>8394</S> is infinite<S>4557</S>. <sup>his: Heb. of his understanding there is no number</sup>
KJV.6: The LORD<S>3068</S> lifteth up<S>5749</S> the meek<S>6035</S>: he casteth<S>8213</S> the wicked<S>7563</S> down<S>8213</S> to the ground<S>776</S>.
KJV.7: Sing<S>6030</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S> with thanksgiving<S>8426</S>; sing praise<S>2167</S> upon the harp<S>3658</S> unto our God<S>430</S>:
KJV.8: Who covereth<S>3680</S> the heaven<S>8064</S> with clouds<S>5645</S>, who prepareth<S>3559</S> rain<S>4306</S> for the earth<S>776</S>, who maketh grass<S>2682</S> to grow<S>6779</S> upon the mountains<S>2022</S>.
KJV.9: He giveth<S>5414</S> to the beast<S>929</S> his food<S>3899</S>, and to the young<S>1121</S> ravens<S>6158</S> which cry<S>7121</S>.
KJV.10: He delighteth<S>2654</S> not in the strength<S>1369</S> of the horse<S>5483</S>: he taketh not pleasure<S>7521</S> in the legs<S>7785</S> of a man<S>376</S>.
KJV.11: The LORD<S>3068</S> taketh pleasure<S>7521</S> in them that fear<S>3373</S> him, in those that hope<S>3176</S> in his mercy<S>2617</S>.
KJV.12: Praise<S>7623</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, O Jerusalem<S>3389</S>; praise<S>1984</S> thy God<S>430</S>, O Zion<S>6726</S>.
KJV.13: For he hath strengthened<S>2388</S> the bars<S>1280</S> of thy gates<S>8179</S>; he hath blessed<S>1288</S> thy children<S>1121</S> within<S>7130</S> thee.
KJV.14: He maketh<S>7760</S> peace<S>7965</S> in thy borders<S>1366</S>, and filleth<S>7646</S> thee with the finest<S>2459</S> of the wheat<S>2406</S>. <sup>He: Heb. Who maketh thy border peace</sup> <sup>finest: Heb. fat of wheat</sup>
KJV.15: He sendeth forth<S>7971</S> his commandment<S>565</S> upon earth<S>776</S>: his word<S>1697</S> runneth<S>7323</S> very swiftly<S>4120</S>.
KJV.16: He giveth<S>5414</S> snow<S>7950</S> like wool<S>6785</S>: he scattereth<S>6340</S> the hoarfrost<S>3713</S> like ashes<S>665</S>.
KJV.17: He casteth forth<S>7993</S> his ice<S>7140</S> like morsels<S>6595</S>: who can stand<S>5975</S> before<S>6440</S> his cold<S>7135</S>?
KJV.18: He sendeth out<S>7971</S> his word<S>1697</S>, and melteth<S>4529</S> them: he causeth his wind<S>7307</S> to blow<S>5380</S>, and the waters<S>4325</S> flow<S>5140</S>.
KJV.19: He sheweth<S>5046</S> his word<S>1697</S> <S>1697</S> unto Jacob<S>3290</S>, his statutes<S>2706</S> and his judgments<S>4941</S> unto Israel<S>3478</S>. <sup>his word: Heb. his words</sup>
KJV.20: He hath not dealt<S>6213</S> so with any nation<S>1471</S>: and as for his judgments<S>4941</S>, they have not known<S>3045</S> them. Praise<S>1984</S> ye the LORD<S>3050</S>.

ESV.1: Praise the LORD! For it is good to sing praises to our God; for it is pleasant,  and a song of praise is fitting.
ESV.2: The LORD builds up Jerusalem; he gathers the outcasts of Israel.
ESV.3: He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
ESV.4: He determines the number of the stars; he gives to all of them their names.
ESV.5: Great is our Lord, and abundant in power; his understanding is beyond measure.
ESV.6: The LORD lifts up the humble;  he casts the wicked to the ground.
ESV.7: Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving; make melody to our God on the lyre!
ESV.8: He covers the heavens with clouds; he prepares rain for the earth; he makes grass grow on the hills.
ESV.9: He gives to the beasts their food, and to the young ravens that cry.
ESV.10: His delight is not in the strength of the horse, nor his pleasure in the legs of a man,
ESV.11: but the LORD takes pleasure in those who fear him, in those who hope in his steadfast love.
ESV.12: Praise the LORD, O Jerusalem! Praise your God, O Zion!
ESV.13: For he strengthens the bars of your gates; he blesses your children within you.
ESV.14: He makes peace in your borders; he fills you with the finest of the wheat.
ESV.15: He sends out his command to the earth; his word runs swiftly.
ESV.16: He gives snow like wool; he scatters frost like ashes.
ESV.17: He hurls down his crystals of ice like crumbs; who can stand before his cold?
ESV.18: He sends out his word, and melts them; he makes his wind blow and the waters flow.
ESV.19: He declares his word to Jacob, his statutes and rules  to Israel.
ESV.20: He has not dealt thus with any other nation; they do not know his rules.  Praise the LORD!

NASB.1: Praise the LORD! For it is good to sing praises to our God; For it is pleasant [and] praise is becoming.
NASB.2: The LORD builds up Jerusalem; He gathers the outcasts of Israel.
NASB.3: He heals the brokenhearted And binds up their wounds.
NASB.4: He counts the number of the stars; He gives names to all of them.
NASB.5: Great is our Lord and abundant in strength; His understanding is infinite.
NASB.6: The LORD supports the afflicted; He brings down the wicked to the ground.
NASB.7: Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving; Sing praises to our God on the lyre,
NASB.8: Who covers the heavens with clouds, Who provides rain for the earth, Who makes grass to grow on the mountains.
NASB.9: He gives to the beast its food, [And] to the young ravens which cry.
NASB.10: He does not delight in the strength of the horse; He does not take pleasure in the legs of a man.
NASB.11: The LORD favors those who fear Him, Those who wait for His lovingkindness.
NASB.12: Praise the LORD, O Jerusalem! Praise your God, O Zion!
NASB.13: For He has strengthened the bars of your gates; He has blessed your sons within you.
NASB.14: He makes peace in your borders; He satisfies you with the finest of the wheat.
NASB.15: He sends forth His command to the earth; His word runs very swiftly.
NASB.16: He gives snow like wool; He scatters the frost like ashes.
NASB.17: He casts forth His ice as fragments; Who can stand before His cold?
NASB.18: He sends forth His word and melts them; He causes His wind to blow and the waters to flow.
NASB.19: He declares His words to Jacob, His statutes and His ordinances to Israel.
NASB.20: He has not dealt thus with any nation; And as for His ordinances, they have not known them. Praise the LORD!

NLT.1:
Praise the LORD!
How good to sing praises to our God!
How delightful and how fitting!
NLT.2:
The LORD is rebuilding Jerusalem
and bringing the exiles back to Israel.
NLT.3:
He heals the brokenhearted
and bandages their wounds.
NLT.4:
He counts the stars
and calls them all by name.
NLT.5:
How great is our Lord! His power is absolute!
His understanding is beyond comprehension!
NLT.6:
The LORD supports the humble,
but he brings the wicked down into the dust.
NLT.7:
Sing out your thanks to the LORD;
sing praises to our God with a harp.
NLT.8:
He covers the heavens with clouds,
provides rain for the earth,
and makes the grass grow in mountain pastures.
NLT.9:
He gives food to the wild animals
and feeds the young ravens when they cry.
NLT.10:
He takes no pleasure in the strength of a horse
or in human might.
NLT.11:
No, the LORD’s delight is in those who fear him,
those who put their hope in his unfailing love.
NLT.12:
Glorify the LORD, O Jerusalem!
Praise your God, O Zion!
NLT.13:
For he has strengthened the bars of your gates
and blessed your children within your walls.
NLT.14:
He sends peace across your nation
and satisfies your hunger with the finest wheat.
NLT.15:
He sends his orders to the world —
how swiftly his word flies!
NLT.16:
He sends the snow like white wool;
he scatters frost upon the ground like ashes.
NLT.17:
He hurls the hail like stones.
Who can stand against his freezing cold?
NLT.18:
Then, at his command, it all melts.
He sends his winds, and the ice thaws.
NLT.19:
He has revealed his words to Jacob,
his decrees and regulations to Israel.
NLT.20:
He has not done this for any other nation;
they do not know his regulations.
Praise the LORD!

WEB.1: Praise Yah, for it is good to sing praises to our God; for it is pleasant and fitting to praise him.
WEB.2: Yahweh builds up Jerusalem. He gathers together the outcasts of Israel.
WEB.3: He heals the broken in heart, and binds up their wounds.
WEB.4: He counts the number of the stars. He calls them all by their names.
WEB.5: Great is our Lord, and mighty in power. His understanding is infinite.
WEB.6: Yahweh upholds the humble. He brings the wicked down to the ground.
WEB.7: Sing to Yahweh with thanksgiving. Sing praises on the harp to our God,
WEB.8: who covers the sky with clouds, who prepares rain for the earth, who makes grass grow on the mountains.
WEB.9: He provides food for the livestock, and for the young ravens when they call.
WEB.10: He doesn’t delight in the strength of the horse. He takes no pleasure in the legs of a man.
WEB.11: Yahweh takes pleasure in those who fear him, in those who hope in his loving kindness.
WEB.12: Praise Yahweh, Jerusalem! Praise your God, Zion!
WEB.13: For he has strengthened the bars of your gates. He has blessed your children within you.
WEB.14: He makes peace in your borders. He fills you with the finest of the wheat.
WEB.15: He sends out his commandment to the earth. His word runs very swiftly.
WEB.16: He gives snow like wool, and scatters frost like ashes.
WEB.17: He hurls down his hail like pebbles. Who can stand before his cold?
WEB.18: He sends out his word, and melts them. He causes his wind to blow, and the waters flow.
WEB.19: He shows his word to Jacob; his statutes and his ordinances to Israel.
WEB.20: He has not done this for just any nation. They don’t know his ordinances. Praise Yah!

AMP.1:
Praise the LORD!
For it is good to sing praises to our [gracious and majestic] God;
Praise is becoming and appropriate.
AMP.2:
The LORD is building up Jerusalem;
He is gathering [together] the exiles of Israel.
AMP.3:
He heals the brokenhearted
And binds up their wounds [healing their pain and comforting their sorrow].
AMP.4:
He counts the number of the stars;
He calls them all by their names.
AMP.5:
Great is our [majestic and mighty] Lord and abundant in strength;
His understanding is inexhaustible [infinite, boundless].
AMP.6:
The LORD lifts up the humble;
He casts the wicked down to the ground.
AMP.7:
Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving;
Sing praises to our God with the lyre,
AMP.8:
Who covers the heavens with clouds,
Who provides rain for the earth,
Who makes grass grow on the mountains.
AMP.9:
He gives to the beast its food,
And to the young ravens that for which they cry.
AMP.10:
He does not delight in the strength (military power) of the horse,
Nor does He take pleasure in the legs (strength) of a man.
AMP.11:
The LORD favors those who fear and worship Him [with awe-inspired reverence and obedience],
Those who wait for His mercy and lovingkindness.
AMP.12:
Praise the LORD, O Jerusalem!
Praise your God, O Zion!
AMP.13:
For He has strengthened the bars of your gates,
He has blessed your children within you.
AMP.14:
He makes peace in your borders;
He satisfies you with the finest of the wheat.
AMP.15:
He sends His command to the earth;
His word runs very swiftly.
AMP.16:
He gives [to the earth] snow like [a blanket of] wool;
He scatters the frost like ashes.
AMP.17:
He casts out His ice like fragments;
Who can stand before His cold?
AMP.18:
He sends out His word and melts the ice;
He causes His wind to blow and the waters to flow.
AMP.19:
He declares His word to Jacob,
His statutes and His ordinances to Israel.
AMP.20:
He has not dealt this way with any [other] nation;
They have not known [understood, appreciated, heeded, or cherished] His ordinances.
Praise the LORD! (Hallelujah!)

MSG.1:
Hallelujah!
It's a good thing to sing praise to our God;
praise is beautiful, praise is fitting.
MSG.2:
God's the one who rebuilds Jerusalem,
who regathers Israel's scattered exiles.
MSG.3:
He heals the heartbroken
and bandages their wounds.
MSG.4:
He counts the stars
and assigns each a name.
MSG.5:
Our Lord is great, with limitless strength;
we'll never comprehend what he knows and does
MSG.6:
God puts the fallen on their feet again
and pushes the wicked into the ditch.
MSG.7:
Sing to God a thanksgiving hymn,
play music on your instruments to God,
MSG.8:
Who fills the sky with clouds,
preparing rain for the earth,
Then turning the mountains green with grass,
MSG.10:
He's not impressed with horsepower;
the size of our muscles means little to him.
MSG.11:
Those who fear God get God's attention;
they can depend on his strength.
MSG.12:
Jerusalem, worship God!
Zion, praise your God!
MSG.13:
He made your city secure,
he blessed your children among you.
MSG.14:
He keeps the peace at your borders,
he puts the best bread on your tables.
MSG.15:
He launches his promises earthward—
how swift and sure they come!
MSG.16:
He spreads snow like a white fleece,
he scatters frost like ashes,
MSG.17:
He broadcasts hail like birdseed—
who can survive his winter?
MSG.18:
Then he gives the command and it all melts;
he breathes on winter—suddenly it's spring!
MSG.19:
He speaks the same way to Jacob,
speaks words that work to Israel.
MSG.20:
He never did this to the other nations;
they never heard such commands.
Hallelujah!

NIV.1:
Psalm 147
Praise the Lord.
How good it is to sing praises to our God,
how pleasant and fitting to praise him!
NIV.2:
The Lord builds up Jerusalem;
he gathers the exiles of Israel.
NIV.3:
He heals the brokenhearted
and binds up their wounds.
NIV.4:
He determines the number of the stars
and calls them each by name.
NIV.5:
Great is our Lord and mighty in power;
his understanding has no limit.
NIV.6:
The Lord sustains the humble
but casts the wicked to the ground.
NIV.7:
Sing to the Lord with thanksgiving;
make music to our God on the harp.
NIV.8:
He covers the sky with clouds;
he supplies the earth with rain
and makes grass grow on the hills.
NIV.9:
He provides food for the cattle
and for the young ravens when they call.
NIV.10:
His pleasure is not in the strength of the horse,
nor his delight in the legs of a man;
NIV.11:
the Lord delights in those who fear him,
who put their hope in his unfailing love.
NIV.12:
Extol the Lord, O Jerusalem;
praise your God, O Zion,
NIV.13:
for he strengthens the bars of your gates
and blesses your people within you.
NIV.14:
He grants peace to your borders
and satisfies you with the finest of wheat.
NIV.15:
He sends his command to the earth;
his word runs swiftly.
NIV.16:
He spreads the snow like wool
and scatters the frost like ashes.
NIV.17:
He hurls down his hail like pebbles.
Who can withstand his icy blast?
NIV.18:
He sends his word and melts them;
he stirs up his breezes, and the waters flow.
NIV.19:
He has revealed his word to Jacob,
his laws and decrees to Israel.
NIV.20:
He has done this for no other nation;
they do not know his laws.
Praise the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
Praise the LORD!
How good it is to sing praises to our God;
for he is gracious, and a song of praise is fitting.
NRSVCE.2:
The LORD builds up Jerusalem;
he gathers the outcasts of Israel.
NRSVCE.3:
He heals the brokenhearted,
and binds up their wounds.
NRSVCE.4:
He determines the number of the stars;
he gives to all of them their names.
NRSVCE.5:
Great is our Lord, and abundant in power;
his understanding is beyond measure.
NRSVCE.6:
The LORD lifts up the downtrodden;
he casts the wicked to the ground.
NRSVCE.7:
Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving;
make melody to our God on the lyre.
NRSVCE.8:
He covers the heavens with clouds,
prepares rain for the earth,
makes grass grow on the hills.
NRSVCE.9:
He gives to the animals their food,
and to the young ravens when they cry.
NRSVCE.10:
His delight is not in the strength of the horse,
nor his pleasure in the speed of a runner;
NRSVCE.11:
but the LORD takes pleasure in those who fear him,
in those who hope in his steadfast love.
NRSVCE.12:
Praise the LORD, O Jerusalem!
Praise your God, O Zion!
NRSVCE.13:
For he strengthens the bars of your gates;
he blesses your children within you.
NRSVCE.14:
He grants peace within your borders;
he fills you with the finest of wheat.
NRSVCE.15:
He sends out his command to the earth;
his word runs swiftly.
NRSVCE.16:
He gives snow like wool;
he scatters frost like ashes.
NRSVCE.17:
He hurls down hail like crumbs —
who can stand before his cold?
NRSVCE.18:
He sends out his word, and melts them;

NRSVCE.19:
He declares his word to Jacob,
his statutes and ordinances to Israel.
NRSVCE.20:

NET.1: Praise the LORD, for it is good to sing praises to our God! Yes, praise is pleasant and appropriate!
NET.2: The LORD rebuilds Jerusalem, and gathers the exiles of Israel.
NET.3: He heals the brokenhearted, and bandages their wounds.
NET.4: He counts the number of the stars; he names all of them.
NET.5: Our Lord is great and has awesome power; there is no limit to his wisdom.
NET.6: The LORD lifts up the oppressed, but knocks the wicked to the ground.
NET.7: Offer to the LORD a song of thanks! Sing praises to our God to the accompaniment of a harp!
NET.8: He covers the sky with clouds, provides the earth with rain, and causes grass to grow on the hillsides.
NET.9: He gives food to the animals, and to the young ravens when they chirp.
NET.10: He is not enamored with the strength of a horse, nor is he impressed by the warrior’s strong legs.
NET.11: The LORD takes delight in his faithful followers, and in those who wait for his loyal love.
NET.12: Extol the LORD, O Jerusalem! Praise your God, O Zion!
NET.13: For he makes the bars of your gates strong. He blesses your children within you.
NET.14: He brings peace to your territory. He abundantly provides for you the best grain.
NET.15: He sends his command through the earth; swiftly his order reaches its destination.
NET.16: He sends the snow that is white like wool; he spreads the frost that is white like ashes.
NET.17: He throws his hailstones like crumbs. Who can withstand the cold wind he sends?
NET.18: He then orders it all to melt; he breathes on it, and the water flows.
NET.19: He proclaims his word to Jacob, his statutes and regulations to Israel.
NET.20: He has not done so with any other nation; they are not aware of his regulations. Praise the LORD!
